attachment.content:
- glm-plan-usage:usage-query: Query the usage information for the current account
- glm-plan-bug:case-feedback: Submit case feedback to report issues or suggestions for the current conversation
- glm-plan-usage:usage-query-skill: Run the usage query script to retrieve account usage information for GLM Coding Plan. Only use when invoked by usage-query-agent.
- glm-plan-bug:case-feedback-skill: Run the case feedback script to submit feedback for the current conversation. Only use when invoked by case-feedback-agent.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll read each of the slide files and report their contents as requested.

content.content:
1 import React, { useRef, memo } from 'react'; 2 import gsap from 'gsap'; 3 import { useGSAP } from '@gsap/react'; 4 import { BookOpen, Zap, Brain } from 'lucide-react'; 5 6 interface SlideProps { isActive: boolean; } 7 8 const COLUMNS = [ 9 { title: '解决知识问题', color: 'var(--success)', icon: BookOpen, items: [ 10 { label: 'Skills = 业务流程的"食谱"', desc: '不只知道事实，还知道怎么做' }, 11 { label: 'SKILL.md 标准', desc: '三部分：元数据+工作流步骤+约束' }, 12 { label: '示例：日报生成Skill', desc: '知道去哪取数据+数据格式+报告模板' }, 13 ]}, 14 { title: '解决行动问题', color: 'var(--accent)', icon: Zap, items: [ 15 { label: 'Skills = 工具的"组合配方"', desc: '编排多个工具完成复杂任务' }, 16 { label: 'Tools vs Skills', desc: '工具是厨具，技能是食谱' }, 17 { label: '示例：日报Skill编排', desc: 'query_database + format + send_email' }, 18 ]}, 19 { title: '解决记忆问题', color: 'var(--primary)', icon: Brain, items: [ 20 { label: 'Skills = 渐进式加载', desc: '只加载当前需要的Skill指令' }, 21 { label: 'Token消耗对比', desc: '2,700 vs 50,000 — 节省94%' }, 22 { label: '任务完成率', desc: '25% → 95%（LangChain实验）' }, 23 ]}, 24 ]; 25 26 const Slide10_OpenClawSkills: React.FC = ({ isActive }) => { 27 const containerRef = useRef(null); 28 29 useGSAP(() => { 30 if (!isActive || !containerRef.current) return; 31 const ctx = gsap.context(() => { 32 gsap.fromTo('.sk-title', { opacity: 0, y: 20 }, { opacity: 1, y: 0, duration: 0.6 }); 33 gsap.fromTo('.sk-col', { opacity: 0, y: 30 }, { opacity: 1, y: 0, duration: 0.5, stagger: 0.2, delay: 0.3 }); 34 gsap.fromTo('.sk-bottom', { opacity: 0, y: 20 }, { opacity: 1, y: 0, duration: 0.5, delay: 1.2 }); 35 }, containerRef); 36 return () => ctx.revert(); 37 }, { scope: containerRef, dependencies: [isActive] }); 38 39 return ( 40

content.text:
## Report of Slide Components

### Slide06_KnowledgeProblem.tsx
- **Component**: `Slide06_KnowledgeProblem`
- **Content**: Knowledge problem presentation showing how models lack knowledge about specific businesses. Demonstrates RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), knowledge graphs, and Skills as solutions.
- **Structure**: Title area, problem flow visualization, 3 solution cards (RAG, knowledge graph, Skills), interactive RAG demo with step-by-step visualization, expandable section explaining when to use each solution.

### Slide07_ActionProblem.tsx
- **Component**: `Slide07_ActionProblem`
- **Content**: Action problem presentation showing models can only generate text, not execute operations. Features MCP (Model Context Protocol) call simulator and MCP vs Skills comparison.
- **Structure**: Title area, MCP call simulator with interactive demo (3-step button progression), comparison cards showing MCP problems vs Skills solutions, expandable section explaining MCP details.

### Slide08_ReAct.tsx
- **Component**: `Slide08_ReAct`
- **Content**: ReAct (Reasoning and Acting) presentation showing how models perform THINK → ACT → OBSERVE cycles to complete complex tasks.
- **Structure**: Title/subtitle, step-by-step visualization with colored cards (Think=blue, Act=orange, Observe=green, Done=gray), "Next Step" button, progress bar, bottom insight explaining the ReAct cycle.

### Slide09_OpenClawArch.tsx
- **Component**: `Slide09_OpenClawArch`
- **Content**: OpenClaw architecture breakdown showing 3 layers (Gateway/Brain, Node/Hands, Channel/Senses) and its open-source status.
- **Structure**: Title with GitHub star counter (animated), 3 layered cards showing architecture components with icons and descriptions, statistics grid (13,729 registered Skills, 100% open source, 20+ platforms), expandable section with Unix philosophy.

### Slide10_OpenClawSkills.tsx
- **Component**: `Slide10_OpenClawSkills`
- **Content**: Skills system explanation showing how Skills bridge knowledge, action, and memory problems in OpenClaw.
- **Structure**: Title, 3-column grid (each with different color) showing how Skills solve: knowledge problems (success/green), action problems (accent/orange), memory problems (primary/blue), bottom note about ClawHub skills marketplace.

### Slide11_OpenClawMemory.tsx
- **Component**: `Slide11_OpenClawMemory`
- **Content**: Four-layer memory system (Session, USER, TOOLS, SOUL) showing how Agents remember everything from identity to real-time context.
- **Structure**: Title, pyramid visualization of 4 memory layers (bottom to top), right side showing memory retrieval process (6 steps), 3 feature cards (pre-compression, semantic search, retrieval latency), expandable section explaining SOUL layer importance.
